2015-06-26.abstract: The Russian president called to talk about the battle against ISIS, while the American president focused on the separatist war in Ukraine.

2015-06-26.title: India: Severe Flooding Kills Dozens
2015-06-26.abstract: At least 41 people have been killed in severe flooding caused by heavy monsoon rains that have lashed part of the western state of Gujarat.

2015-06-26.title: French Authorities Hold Suspect in Beheading and Explosion at Chemical Plant
2015-06-26.abstract: President François Hollande said a beheaded body had been found at the American-operated factory, and that the assailant had been arrested and identified.

2015-06-27.title: Mass Killings by ISIS Fighters in Syrian Kurdish Town
2015-06-27.abstract: Islamic State fighters who sneaked into the Syrian Kurdish town of Kobani on the Turkish border killed more than 150 people there and in nearby villages.

2015-06-27.title: Working Within the System to Disrupt Brazilian Politics
2015-06-27.abstract: Roberto Mangabeira Unger, a Harvard philosopher born in Brazil and raised in Manhattan, has settled into a career as an appointed public figure charged with fostering long-term thinking about Brazil.

2015-06-27.title: Nepal: Rough Terrain and Bad Weather Led to Helicopter Crash, Report Says
2015-06-27.abstract: The crash of a United States Marines helicopter in Nepal that killed 13 people in May was caused by bad weather and the geography of the region, the Nepalese Army said.

2015-06-27.title: Yevgeny Primakov, Former Russian Premier and Spymaster, Dies at 85
2015-06-27.abstract: A former prime minister and intelligence chief, Mr. Primakov was also the Kremlin’s top expert on Middle Eastern affairs.

2015-06-27.title: Greek Debt Crisis Intensifies as Extension Request Is Denied
2015-06-27.abstract: European officials rejected Greece’s request to extend its bailout program so voters could decide whether to accept aid under terms the government opposes.

2015-06-28.title: Taiwan Water Park Blast Leaves Hundreds Injured
2015-06-28.abstract: No deaths were reported, but at least 516 people were injured when a cloud of colored powder erupted into a huge fireball during a live event at the park northwest of Taipei.

2015-06-28.title: Hundreds Treated for Burns After Fire at Taiwan Water Park
2015-06-28.abstract: The public’s focus has turned to how airborne powder being sprayed at a “color party” could have ignited on an overwhelmingly young crowd.

2015-06-28.title: 1940: Hong Kong Civilians Prepare To Leave
2015-06-28.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: Britain planned to evacuate the wives and children of British army and naval officers from Hong Kong in 1940.

2015-06-28.title: 1965: Dutch Princess To Marry German Diplomat
2015-06-28.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: The announcement of a royal engagement caused an uproar in the Netherlands in 1965.

2015-06-28.title: Afghan President Appoints a Second Female Governor
2015-06-28.abstract: Even though his first appointee has been unable to take office because of protests, President Ashraf Ghani picked another woman as a provincial leader.

2015-06-29.title: Tunisian Gunman Showed Subtle Signs of Radicalization
2015-06-29.abstract: Seifeddine Rezgui, the Tunisian student who shot 39 tourists at a seaside hotel on Friday, was a break-dancer in his youth but turned to extremism while in college.

2015-06-29.title: China and India Are Sitting Out Refugee Crisis
2015-06-29.abstract: Asia’s giants could play leadership roles but see it as a losing issue that could diminish their clout and bilateral interests.

2015-06-29.title: French Assailant Texted Beheading Photo to Canadian Number
2015-06-29.abstract: French authorities said Yassine Salhi, 35, accused in the beheading and an explosion at an American chemical factory in southeastern France, used a smartphone app to send a photo of himself with the victim’s head.

2015-06-29.title: Greece Will Shut Banks in Fallout From Debt Crisis
2015-06-29.abstract: The prime minister’s decisions escalated tensions and uncertainty over a financial crisis that some analysts say could have global ripples.

2015-06-29.title: Greece to Close Banks to Stem Tide of Withdrawals
2015-06-29.abstract: Greece will keep its banks closed on Monday, Prime Minister Alexis Tsipras said in a televised address on Sunday night.

2015-06-30.title: U.S. Tells Iran That Preliminary Nuclear Deal Must Stand
2015-06-30.abstract: A senior American official declined to say if the United States might agree to adjust some elements of the Lausanne framework in return for new Iranian concessions.

2015-06-30.title: Peace Eludes South Sudan as Rivals Hold Talks
2015-06-30.abstract: A spokesman for the rebels said that “progress is being made” in negotiations to end South Sudan’s civil war, even as forces had seized the strategic city of Malakal.

2015-06-30.title: Russia: Officials Meet on Syrian Crisis
2015-06-30.abstract: Russia’s foreign minister said Monday that he would meet Tuesday with Secretary of State John Kerry to discuss the crisis in Syria and rising attacks by extremists.

2015-06-30.title: U.N. Council Extends Troops’ Presence in Darfur
2015-06-30.abstract: The council agreed unanimously to keep peacekeepers in Sudan’s Darfur region for at least another year, but did not reach a consensus on how to help gain custody of Sudan’s president.

2015-06-30.title: Israel to Build Fence Along at Least Part of Jordan Frontier
2015-06-30.abstract: The plan will move Israel closer to completely surrounding itself with physical barriers.
